name: Teck Resources Limited

profile.description: Teck Resources Limited is a leading Canadian mining company focused on copper and zinc production, with principal operations in the Americas. Major assets include Quebrada Blanca (Chile), Highland Valley Copper (Canada), Antamina (Peru), and Trail zinc smelting operations (Canada). The company divested its steelmaking coal business in 2023 to become a pure-play critical minerals producer for the energy transition. Currently pursuing a merger of equals with Anglo American plc to create a global critical minerals champion. Headquartered in Vancouver, British Columbia.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Teck executed two transformative strategic moves — divesting its entire steelmaking coal business (previously the majority of profits) to become a pure copper and zinc producer, and the $5.2B Quebrada Blanca Phase 2 expansion that is now delivering record copper production of 140,000 tonnes in Q1 2026 [1][2]. The Anglo American merger of equals creates a top-5 global copper producer. These represent genuine strategic transformation. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI-powered products or quantified AI-driven cost savings. The combined Anglo Teck entity plans to investigate AI applications in mineral exploration, but no current deployment exists [2]. Market Share (1/2): Meaningful copper producer but not dominant — copper production guidance of 455-530K tonnes for 2026 places Teck among top-10 global producers but behind Codelco, Freeport-McMoRan, and BHP [1]. The Anglo American merger would elevate position to top-5. Product Pipeline (1/2): QB Phase 2 ramp continues with record production. Anglo American merger pipeline includes copper growth projects. 2026 copper production guidance of 455-530K tonnes provides visibility [1]. However, mining projects inherently carry geological and permitting uncertainty. R&D Intensity (0/1): Mining companies invest in exploration and process innovation but not at levels comparable to technology or pharma peers relative to revenue.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Teck's purpose centers on 'developing resources for a better world' with emphasis on responsible mining for the energy transition [2]. Clear and directional — the pivot from coal to copper directly embodies this mission. However, the purpose is generic for a mining company positioning itself for the energy transition era. It identifies what the company does but doesn't articulate a deeper vision beyond responsible resource extraction. Community Impact (1/2): 'Nature positive by 2030' pledge (conserving 3 hectares for each hectare affected by mining) and industry-leading ESG ratings (S&P CSA leader in Metals & Mining, Moody's #1 in North American metals & mining) demonstrate genuine commitment [3]. However, the legacy of Elk Valley environmental issues in British Columbia tempers the narrative. Employee Retention (1/2): Leadership emphasizes empowerment, innovation, and performance culture where 'new ideas are actively encouraged at all levels' with paths to fund innovative ideas outside normal budget processes [2]. Jonathan Price has been CEO since 2023. No specific retention metrics available. Insider Buying (0/2): No notable insider buying transactions identified in the last 12 months.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored as Growth sleeve (dividend yield 0.86% below 2% threshold). D/E of 37% is well below the 70% target, scoring a perfect 1.0 — the company ended Q1 2026 in a net cash position of $488M [1][4]. Forward P/E of 16.39 is below the 20 target, scoring 1.0 [4]. Revenue growth YoY of 16.4% ($7.698B FY2025 vs $6.616B FY2024) exceeds the 10% target [1]. Key weaknesses: ROE of 3.06% is well below the 15% target (scoring 0.20) due to the large equity base and depressed 2024 earnings from the coal divestiture transition [4]. Profit margin of 11.98% is below the 20% target (scoring 0.60) [4]. 5-year revenue CAGR of approximately -2.3% (2021 $10.1B to TTM March 2026 $8.977B) is distorted by the coal business divestiture and scores 0.18 — the underlying copper business is growing rapidly [1]. Forward PEG of approximately 1.1 is near the 1.0 target, scoring 0.95.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (9/9): Mining is a fundamentally physical-world operation — extracting copper and zinc from the earth requires massive capital-intensive infrastructure, heavy equipment, geological expertise, regulatory permits, and environmental compliance that AI structurally cannot displace [2]. Teck is maximally immune to AI disruption. Moreover, AI dramatically increases demand for copper — every AI data center requires approximately 2-3x more copper wiring than traditional data centers, electric vehicles require 3-4x more copper than ICE vehicles, and renewable energy infrastructure is copper-intensive [1]. Record copper prices of $5.78/lb in Q1 2026 are partly driven by AI infrastructure demand. Teck's copper production directly enables AI buildout. AI Opportunity (0/1): No AI-generated revenue. Teck produces the physical commodity that AI infrastructure consumes, but does not sell AI products or services.

infinite_game.summary: Teck Resources demonstrates significant infinite game characteristics through its courageous coal-to-copper transformation and the Anglo American merger, both driven by conviction about long-term relevance in the energy transition era. However, the company's relative youth under current leadership (CEO Price since 2023), the profit-driven nature of resource extraction, and the lack of a deeply validated Just Cause beyond industry positioning prevent a higher score. The coal divestiture is a Sinek-worthy example of existential flexibility, but it remains to be seen whether the purpose extends beyond smart strategic positioning.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): 'Developing resources for a better world' articulates a purpose but reads as industry positioning rather than a deeply held cause that would survive leadership changes and crises [2]. The coal-to-copper pivot was brilliant strategy aligned with the energy transition, but it was equally motivated by valuation multiple expansion (copper companies trade at higher multiples than coal). The purpose hasn't been tested through extended periods where pursuing it was costly or unpopular.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Teck emphasizes building a culture of empowerment, innovation, and performance, with leadership encouraging new ideas at all levels and providing funding paths outside normal budget processes [2]. CEO Jonathan Price has been in role since 2023, providing recent continuity. However, mining companies typically operate with hierarchical structures, and the Anglo American merger introduces significant organizational uncertainty.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): No evidence of infinite competitive mindset in management commentary. The Anglo American merger is framed as creating a 'global critical minerals champion' — language focused on scale and leadership rather than self-improvement [2]. Mining industry competition is inherently resource-based (securing deposits and permits) rather than capability-based, leaving less room for the worthy rival dynamic.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The divestiture of the entire steelmaking coal business — which was Teck's largest and most profitable segment at the time — to become a pure copper and zinc producer is a textbook example of existential flexibility [2][3]. The company abandoned its most profitable business line in service of long-term relevance in the energy transition. The Anglo American merger doubles down on this conviction. This is comparable to Sinek's examples of companies reinventing themselves at material cost to the near-term business.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): The coal divestiture was a genuinely courageous decision — walking away from the company's most profitable segment when many peers were content to harvest coal profits [2][3]. Management accepted significant near-term earnings reduction (2023 revenue plunged 64% from $13.3B to $4.8B) in service of long-term strategic positioning. The Anglo American merger of equals is another bold move, diluting control to 37.6% ownership for access to world-class copper growth assets. Both decisions prioritize long-term capability over current earnings.

investment_thesis: Teck Resources is executing a compelling transformation from diversified miner to pure-play copper producer at precisely the right moment in the copper supercycle. Q1 2026 delivered record results — revenue surged to $3.94B (from $2.29B in Q1 2025), adjusted EBITDA more than doubled to $2.1B, and copper segment revenue jumped 92% to $2.9B on record QB production and copper prices of $5.78/lb [1]. The company ended Q1 in a net cash position of $488M with D/E of just 37%. The Anglo American merger of equals will create a top-5 global copper producer with over 70% copper exposure and a robust pipeline of growth projects [2]. Copper demand fundamentals are structurally positive — AI data centers, electric vehicles, and renewable energy all require significantly more copper than conventional alternatives.
thesis_continued: The primary risk is commodity price cyclicality. Copper prices of $5.78/lb are near all-time highs, and any normalization could severely compress earnings — the TTM ROE of 3.06% reflects how quickly mining margins can evaporate in unfavorable price environments [4]. The 5-year revenue CAGR of -2.3% (distorted by coal divestiture) and trailing P/E of 27.80 suggest the market is pricing in continued elevated copper prices. The Anglo American merger introduces execution risk — integration of a company of similar scale with operations across multiple continents is inherently complex, and Teck shareholders will own only 37.6% of the combined entity [2]. ESG controversies around the legacy coal business and Elk Valley environmental issues persist despite the divestiture [3]. CEO Jonathan Price has been in role only since 2023, creating leadership uncertainty during a transformative period. Profit margin of 11.98% reflects the capital-intensive nature of mining and leaves limited earnings cushion against copper price declines.
key_catalysts: Anglo American merger of equals creating a top-5 global copper producer with 70%+ copper exposure and world-class growth project pipeline, expected to close in 2026-2027
Quebrada Blanca Phase 2 production ramp to full capacity of 300K+ tonnes annually, with Q1 2026 already delivering record 70,300-tonne quarterly sales
Structural copper demand growth from AI data center buildout (2-3x copper intensity), electric vehicle adoption, and renewable energy infrastructure supporting sustained above-trend pricing
key_risks: Copper price cyclicality — realized price of $5.78/lb is near all-time highs, and normalization to $3.50-4.00/lb would dramatically compress margins given the capital-intensive cost structure

Anglo American merger execution risk — integrating a company of similar scale across multiple continents with 37.6% Teck shareholder ownership creates dilution, cultural integration, and operational challenges

5-year revenue CAGR of -2.3% and trailing P/E of 27.80 reflect a company priced for continued copper supercycle — any demand slowdown from China or delayed AI infrastructure buildout could trigger multiple compression
